title: Project Scaffolding
description: Set up initial project structure: pyproject.toml, .claude-plugin/, CLAUDE.md, initial commit.
close_reason: Initial scaffolding complete, pushed to GitHub

title: Log debug session frequency per file
description: Track files where debugger is frequently attached. Debug frequency indicates hard-to-understand or opaque code.

title: Future: Full forensic output from gmap
description: Leverage gmap's full forensic output capabilities for richer analysis. Currently using gmap export --json for basic churn/coupling/ownership.

title: Root cause cluster visualization
description: Force-directed graph of similar root causes. Cluster by semantic similarity. Size = occurrence count.

title: Track time spent per file (active editing)
description: Capture cumulative editing time per file from IDE. Long sessions indicate complexity. Must be opt-in.

title: Coupling graph: top-N filtering with directory focus
description: Redesign coupling graph to be readable by default. Show top 10 edges instead of threshold-based filtering. Add slider (10-100) and click-to-focus interactions. See docs/plans/2026-01-26-coupling-graph-redesign.md
close_reason: Implemented top-N filtering with slider, click-to-focus, and tooltip

title: Detect revert PRs and flag unstable changes
description: Identify PRs that revert previous PRs (title pattern: Revert, revert commit messages). Flag files that appear in reverts as high-risk. Track revert rate per file.

title: Coupling graph: show threshold value in empty state
description: When no coupling above threshold exists, empty state just says 'no files found'. Could show the threshold value (0.3) to help users understand why.

title: File hotspot heatmap visualization
description: Visual heatmap of files by issue count. Color intensity = problem severity. Drill down to see specific GOTCHAs/BUGs.

title: Engine repair: correct forensic signals
description: Fix the correctness of every forensic signal before any new surface consumes them. Covers: native git extraction (drop gmap), repo_path threading, indentation complexity proxy (true hotspot formula), bug-fix commit linkage, flaky detection rewrite against real GitHub API semantics, loud degraded modes, docs truth pass. Plan: .planning/superpowers/plans/2026-06-12-agent-native-pivot.md Milestone A.

title: Agent-native surface: MCP server + plugin v1.0
description: Expose the corrected forensic signals as agent context: FastMCP server (bbu-mcp) with six read tools, rewritten Claude Code plugin that actually invokes bbu and registers the MCP server, v1.0.0 release. HTML report frozen. Plan: .planning/superpowers/plans/2026-06-12-agent-native-pivot.md Milestone B. Must not start until engine repair (BBU-6336) is complete.

title: Native git history extraction (git/log.py)
description: fetch_git_history(repo_path, days) via git log --numstat --pretty, producing the existing entries dict shape plus a message field. Raises NotAGitRepoError / GitToolNotFoundError. Plan Task 1.

title: Swap pipeline to native git, remove gmap
description: analysis.py and churn.py consume fetch_git_history; delete _fetch_gmap_data and gmap subprocess; rename parse_gmap_output->parse_history_entries, parse_ownership_from_gmap->parse_ownership_from_history; drop requires_gmap test machinery; remove gmap from README. Plan Task 2.

title: Deterministic fixture-repo integration test
description: Replace the wall-clock-dependent churn integration test (asserts commits in last 30 days of this repo) with a tmp_path fixture repo built via git init + two commits. Runs in CI now that gmap is gone. Plan Task 3.

title: Thread repo_path through gh/git subprocesses, add --repo flag
description: All cicd subprocess calls gain cwd=repo_path; _fetch_ci_failures(repo_path); CLI gains --repo. Today gh/git show resolve from process cwd while history uses repo_path - analyzing another repo silently mixes two repositories. Plan Task 4.

title: Indentation complexity proxy + true hotspot score
description: complexity.py: sum of logical indentation levels (Tornhill's whitespace method). FileForensics.complexity field; hotspot_score = commits x complexity (currently commits x lines_changed = churn x churn, mislabeled as Tornhill's formula at models.py:129). Plan Task 5.

title: Defect linkage: bug-fix commit detection per file
description: git/defects.py: is_bugfix_message regex (fix/bug/hotfix/revert/regression) + bugfix_counts(history); FileForensics.bugfix_commits. Makes the '2-8% of files cause 60-90% of defects' pitch measurable - today no signal touches defect data. Supersedes PR-level revert detection (BBU-4509). Plan Task 6.

title: Flaky detection rewrite against real GitHub API semantics
description: Old detector groups on workflow_name (field is 'name' in /actions/runs) and assumes re-runs are separate list entries; GitHub mutates runs in place with run_attempt. New: jobs?filter=all per re-run run only (kills N+1, supersedes BBU-eao), pure flaky_steps_from_jobs, drop the >10%-failure mislabeling branch. Plan Task 7.

title: Wire flaky steps into pipeline; loguru warnings for degraded CI
description: AnalysisResult.flaky_steps has been a dead field since introduction. Populate via _fetch_flaky_steps; replace silent except-Exception-return-empty in _fetch_ci_failures with loguru warnings so gh auth failure is no longer indistinguishable from green CI; give --verbose real output. Plan Task 8.

title: Docs truth pass: ARCHITECTURE.md, README, CLAUDE.md
description: ARCHITECTURE.md describes six modules that do not exist and a D3 stack never built - rewrite to reality. Fix README ARCHITECTURE link path, signal formulas everywhere, replace plugin-first claim with agent-first (MCP+plugin). Plan Task 9.

title: MCP server: bbu-mcp with six read tools
description: FastMCP stdio server over run_analysis: get_hotspots, get_file_forensics, get_coupled_files, get_ownership, get_ci_failures, get_flaky_steps; per-(repo,days) cache; bbu-mcp console script; mcp dependency. Plan Task 10.

title: Plugin rewrite: commands invoke bbu, register bbu-mcp
description: Current plugin (v0.1.0) never calls bbu - raw git one-liners with a third scoring formula. Rewrite analyze/hotspots commands to consume bbu JSON, rewrite git-forensics agent, add .claude-plugin/.mcp.json registering bbu-mcp, version 1.0.0. Plan Task 11.

title: Release v1.0.0
description: Version bump (pyproject, __init__, plugin already 1.0.0), CHANGELOG 1.0.0 section, uv lock, full verification (pytest, ruff, real bbu run showing hotspot+bugfix values), tag v1.0.0, push, confirm CI green. Plan Task 12.

title: Upstream PR to code-review-graph: churn term in compute_risk_score
description: Separate repo (fork of tirth8205/code-review-graph at ~/VSCode/code-review-graph, 262 commits behind upstream). Propose an optional churn weight in compute_risk_score (changes.py:151-201) sourced from native git log - no bbu/gmap dependency, upstream-acceptable. Delivers hotspot-weighted review prioritization inside the MCP server already used daily. If upstream declines, do NOT carry a fork - drop it.

title: Function-level forensics (Tornhill X-Ray): per-function churn
description: v1.1 headline: per-function churn via diff hunk parsing - the largest analytical upgrade available and the one CodeScene paywalls. Big lift (function boundary detection; consider indentation heuristics before tree-sitter). Start with a design/discuss cycle, not code. Context: .planning/superpowers/plans/2026-06-12-agent-native-pivot.md (v1.1 candidates footer).

title: Self-validation: hotspot-rank vs bugfix-density correlation
description: With bugfix linkage shipped (FileForensics.bugfix_commits), compute the correlation between hotspot rank and bug-fix density across several real repos and publish the number in the README - the tool proving its own pitch. Small: a script or bbu subcommand + doc.

title: Chore: guard top-3 deterministic tiebreak; refresh artifact action SHAs
description: Two ten-minute items from v1.0.0 final review: (1) guard.py top-3 selection has arbitrary order among equal ratios - add secondary sort by path + test; (2) upload/download-artifact v4 SHAs emit Node-20 deprecation warnings (forced Node 24 from 2026-06-16) - bump to current SHAs in .github/workflows/release.yml.

title: Initial commit and push to GitHub
description: Commit all scaffolding and push to remote
close_reason: Initial scaffolding complete, pushed to GitHub

title: Extract file churn from git log
description: Parse git log to count commits per file. High churn = instability. Output: {file: path, commits: N, lines_changed: N}
close_reason: Implemented file churn extraction with gmap, 11 tests passing

title: Future: AST-based complexity analysis
description: Add true complexity metrics using AST parsing. Consider using tree-sitter or language-specific parsers. Current proxy is lines_changed from churn data.

title: flush check (delete me)
Track rollback rate per deploy
description: Identify deploys that get rolled back. Map to files changed in those deploys. Rollbacks are production failure signals.

title: Calculate PR cycle time per file
description: Measure time from PR open to merge. Aggregate by file touched. Long cycle times indicate friction areas - files that are hard to review or contentious.

title: Create CLAUDE.md
description: Project instructions for Claude Code
close_reason: Initial scaffolding complete, pushed to GitHub

title: Coupling graph: expand color palette beyond 10 colors
description: HTML report uses a hardcoded 10-color palette for directory grouping. Repos with 11+ directories reuse colors, reducing visual distinction.

title: Track build failures per file changed
description: Parse CI logs to identify which files appear in commits that break builds. Correlate with file churn to find fragile code.
close_reason: Implemented CI/CD build failure tracking with gh CLI, HTML column, --no-ci flag

title: Create pyproject.toml
description: Python project with uv, black-box-unlock package
close_reason: Initial scaffolding complete, pushed to GitHub

title: Create .claude-plugin/ structure
description: plugin.json, commands/, hooks/, agents/ directories
close_reason: Initial scaffolding complete, pushed to GitHub

title: Parse JUnit XML artifacts for test-level flakiness
Investigate batch fetching for GitHub Actions data (reduce API calls)
Temporal coupling graph
description: Network graph showing files that change together. Node size = churn, edge thickness = coupling strength. D3.js or similar.
close_reason: Implemented Cytoscape.js network graph for temporal coupling visualization

title: Detect temporal coupling from commits
description: Find files that change together. If A and B change in same commit u003e30% of time = hidden dependency. Tornhill's logical coupling.
close_reason: Implemented temporal coupling detection with TDD: 8 unit tests, Tornhill's min-based formula, 30% threshold filtering

title: Coupling graph: scale node size by churn
description: Coupling graph currently uses uniform node sizes. Scaling node size by churn would provide additional visual signal about file importance.

title: Calculate ownership spread per file
description: Count unique authors per file. Many authors = coordination overhead, knowledge diffusion. Flag files with u003e3 authors and high churn.
close_reason: Implemented file ownership calculation with TDD: 11 tests, >3 authors = high risk, reuses gmap data

title: Capture navigation patterns (file→file jumps)
description: Track which files developers navigate between. Reveals hidden coupling and mental models. Aggregate only, no keystroke logging.

title: Decision timeline visualization
description: Interactive timeline of architectural decisions. Filter by topic. Show reversals and evolutions. Swimlanes by component.

title: Epic: GitHub PR/Issue Forensics
description: Extract forensic signals from GitHub PR metadata and issue labels via gh CLI. Complements git-only analysis with human judgment signals (labels), friction metrics (cycle time), and failure indicators (reverts).

title: Epic: CI/CD Metrics Forensics
description: Extract forensic signals from build/test/deploy pipelines. Tracks build failures, flaky tests, deploy frequency, and rollback rates to identify fragile code and risky changes.

title: Track search/find-in-files patterns
description: Capture what developers search for in codebase. Reveals missing documentation, unclear naming, and discoverability issues.

title: CLI: bbu analyze-repo
description: Run git forensics on current repo. Output hotspots combining churn + complexity proxy (file size). Store findings as reference memories.
close_reason: Implemented analyze-repo CLI with JSON and HTML output formats, 54 tests passing

title: Detect flaky tests and link to source files
description: Identify tests that intermittently fail. Map to source files they test. Flaky tests indicate unreliable coverage or race conditions.
close_reason: Implemented step-level flaky detection via gh api REST endpoints

title: Extract review comment density per file
description: Count review comments per PR, aggregate by file. High comment density indicates complex or unclear code that generates discussion.

title: Create logo and favicon
Document project architecture
description: Create ARCHITECTURE.md with full project structure, module organization, data models, and implementation phases. Update CLAUDE.md to reference the architecture doc.
close_reason: Created ARCHITECTURE.md with full project structure, data models, and implementation roadmap. Updated CLAUDE.md to reference it.

title: Git history analysis: Bootstrap from repo forensics
description: Import code forensics from git history: file churn, temporal coupling, ownership patterns. Provides baseline analysis before any handoffs. Based on 'Your Code as a Crime Scene' methodology.
close_reason: Core git forensics implemented: churn, coupling, ownership analysis with JSON/HTML output

title: Parse pytest logs for test-level flakiness (Python projects)
Rich visualizations for code analysis
description: Interactive visualizations: file hotspot heatmaps, temporal coupling graphs, decision timelines, root cause clusters. Dashboard integration.

title: Extract PR labels and categorize work types
description: Use gh pr list with --json to extract labels from merged PRs. Map labels to work categories (bug, feature, enhancement, tech-debt). Track work type distribution over time per file.

title: Epic: IDE Telemetry Forensics
description: Capture developer behavior signals from IDE interactions. Tracks time spent, navigation patterns, debug sessions, and search patterns to reveal cognitive load and hidden coupling. Opt-in only.

title: Calculate deploy frequency per component
description: Track how often code ships per file/component. High-churn areas that deploy frequently may need more attention.
